engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 508 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 508 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced 2018 for Gen 2 508, replacing DV6 with improved emissions and efficiency while maintaining BlueHDi reliability.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.5 BlueHDi, Active Premium 1.5 BlueHDi, Allure 1.5 BlueHDi, Allure Premium 1.5 BlueHDi, GT 1.5 BlueHDi
commonFailures: AdBlue injector crystallisation (typical failure 70k-90k miles)
EGR valve clogging on short-journey driving
Timing chain tensioner wear (recall item on early units)
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5 Peugeot 508 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Workhorse diesel for Gen 1 508, widely used across PSA group with proven durability when maintained.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.6 HDi, Allure 1.6 HDi, Style 1.6 HDi, SW Active 1.6 HDi, SW Allure 1.6 HDi
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking (common 80k-110k miles)
DPF regeneration failure on urban use
Turbo oil seal leak on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6 Peugeot 508 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Robust 2.0L HDi for Gen 1 508, favoured for towing and high-mileage duty with strong torque delivery.
compatibleVariants: Allure 2.0 HDi, GT 2.0 HDi, RXH Hybrid4, SW Allure 2.0 HDi, SW GT 2.0 HDi

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear (90k-120k miles)
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10 Peugeot 508 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L BlueHDi
entries.history: Gen 2 evolution of DW10 with SCR/AdBlue system for Euro 6 compliance, maintaining torque while reducing emissions.
compatibleVariants: Allure 2.0 BlueHDi, Allure Premium 2.0 BlueHDi, GT 2.0 BlueHDi, GT Premium 2.0 BlueHDi, SW GT 2.0 BlueHDi
commonFailures: AdBlue tank/pump assembly failure (60k-85k miles)
SCR catalyst dosing fault triggering limp mode
EGR cooler carbon buildup on stop-start driving
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10FC Peugeot 508 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Early THP turbo petrol for Gen 1 508, delivering sporty performance but requiring careful maintenance of timing components.
compatibleVariants: GT 1.6 THP, GT Line 1.6 THP, First Edition 1.6 THP, Allure 1.6 THP
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (critical 70k-90k miles)
Turbo oil feed pipe leak causing smoke

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear on direct injection units
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6DT Peugeot 508 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L PureTech Turbo
entries.history: Refined Gen 2 PureTech turbo replacing THP, with improved reliability and efficiency while retaining responsive performance.
compatibleVariants: Allure 1.6 PureTech, GT Line 1.6 PureTech, GT 1.6 PureTech, GT Premium 1.6 PureTech, PSE Hybrid (base unit)
commonFailures: HPFP pressure loss causing misfire (80k-100k miles)
Turbo wastegate actuator sticking
Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6FDT Peugeot 508 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L PureTech Turbo
entries.history: Entry-level turbo petrol for Gen 2 508, offering efficiency with adequate performance for urban and motorway use.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.2 PureTech, Allure 1.2 PureTech, SW Active 1.2 PureTech
commonFailures: Timing belt degradation in oil bath (critical 100k miles/10 years)
Oil pump wear on early production units
Turbocharger bearing wear on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2ADTS Peugeot 508 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Naturally Aspirated Petrol
entries.history: Reliable naturally aspirated petrol for early Gen 1 508, simple design with proven longevity when serviced regularly.
commonFailures: Coil pack failure causing misfire (80k+ miles)
Water pump seal leak on high-mileage units
Variable valve timing solenoid sticking
entries.cta: Get quotes for EW10A Peugeot 508 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Hybrid Diesel
entries.history: PSA's pioneering diesel-electric hybrid for RXH crossover variant, combining HDi efficiency with electric rear axle torque.

entries.title: voltage battery cooling system fault (50k-75k miles)
DC converter failure triggering hybrid system warning

entries.history: Gen 2 plug-in hybrid system pairing PureTech turbo with front electric motor for 50km electric range and reduced emissions.

board charger fault limiting electric range (40k-65k miles)

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 508 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why EP6DT/EP6FDT Petrol Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 508 GT 1.6 THP, GT Line 1.6 PureTech, First Edition 1.6 THP (2011-2024, EP6DT & EP6FDT)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure allowing chain slack, causing rattling noise and potential valve interference on interference-design engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, chain; reset ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before noise; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Cylinder Head Inspection + Chain Kit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head to inspect valves; replace damaged components + full chain system
repairOptions.longevity: Appropriate if early valve contact suspected; adds cost uncertainty

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EP6 unit with upgraded tensioner, tested on dyno, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when valves damaged or engine has 90k+ miles; eliminates repeat risk

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 508 worth £8,000-£15,000, preventative chain replacement at 70k miles offers better value than waiting for failure. If rattling is present or compression test shows valve damage, a rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner is the safer long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP6FDT engine prices for your Peugeot 508

problems.h4: AdBlue System Fault - Why DW10FC BlueHDi Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 508 Allure 2.0 BlueHDi, GT 2.0 BlueHDi, SW GT 2.0 BlueHDi (2018-2024, DW10FC)
problems.rootCause: AdBlue injector crystallises or pump assembly fails, triggering SCR system fault and forcing engine into reduced-power limp mode to meet emissions compliance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injector, clean crystallisation or replace unit; reset SCR adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early before pump damage; may recur on short journeys

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace complete tank/pump module; refill AdBlue; perform SCR calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; recommended if injector fault recurs or pump shows wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt DW10FC with Updated SCR Components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded AdBlue injector and SCR calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when engine has other wear items or 100k+ miles; eliminates repeat SCR issues

problems.recommendation: For a Peugeot 508 GT 2.0 BlueHDi valued at £12,000-£18,000, replacing the AdBlue tank/pump assembly is cost-effective if the engine is otherwise healthy. If the vehicle has exceeded 100k miles or shows additional diesel wear signs, a rebuilt DW10FC with updated SCR components offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DW10FC Peugeot 508 engine replacement

problems.h4: DPF Regeneration Failure - Why HDi/BlueHDi Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 508 Active 1.6 HDi, Allure 2.0 HDi, SW 2.0 BlueHDi (2011-2024, DV6/DV5/DW10/DW10FC)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents active DPF regeneration, causing soot accumulation that blocks exhaust flow and triggers power-limiting fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic-led forced DPF burn; clean EGR valve; reset adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Works if blockage is mild and driving patterns change; may need repeating

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF; thermal/chemical clean; pressure test; reinstall with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for moderate blockage; extends DPF life 30k-50k miles if driving habits improve

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Diesel Engine with DPF Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt HDi/BlueHDi unit; include DPF clean or replacement; 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when engine shows additional wear (turbo, injectors) or DPF is beyond cleaning

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 508 used primarily for motorway driving, a professional DPF clean often resolves the issue cost-effectively. For urban-use vehicles with recurring DPF warnings, a rebuilt diesel engine with updated EGR and DPF components provides more reliable long-term ownership.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV5 Peugeot 508 diesel engine prices

problems.h4: 1.2L PureTech Timing Belt Failure - Why EB2ADTS Engines Risk Catastrophic Damage
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 508 Active 1.2 PureTech, Allure 1.2 PureTech (2020-2024, EB2ADTS)
problems.rootCause: Timing belt runs in oil bath; rubber degradation from fuel dilution or extended service intervals causes belt teeth to shear, leading to valve-piston collision on interference engine.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Belt + Oil Pump Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, oil pump; flush oil system; reset service counter
repairOptions.longevity: Critical preventative measure; must be done at 100k miles/10 years regardless of symptoms

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Cylinder Head Rebuild After Belt Failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head; replace bent valves, guides, seals; machine surfaces; reassemble with new belt kit
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block and pistons undamaged; adds uncertainty and cost versus full rebuild

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EB2ADTS with Updated Belt System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.2 PureTech with latest belt specification, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when belt has failed or engine exceeds 100k miles; eliminates repeat risk with upgraded components

problems.recommendation: For any EB2ADTS-equipped Peugeot 508, preventative belt replacement at 100k miles is non-negotiable. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt engine with the latest belt specification offers more predictable cost and reliability than a head-only repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EB2ADTS Peugeot 508 engine replacement

problems.h4: Turbocharger Oil Seal Leak - Why EP6DT/EP6FDT Petrol Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 508 GT 1.6 THP, GT Line 1.6 PureTech, Allure 1.6 THP (2011-2024, EP6DT & EP6FDT)
problems.rootCause: Turbo bearing wear allows oil to leak into intake or exhaust, causing blue smoke, increased oil consumption, and potential catalytic converter damage from oil burn.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Oil Feed/Return Pipe Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace degraded oil lines; clean intake; reset adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if turbo bearings still sound; may delay but not prevent eventual turbo replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old turbo; fit new/remanufactured unit; calibrate wastegate; road test
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if engine otherwise healthy; adds cost of labour and ancillary parts

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EP6 with Upgraded Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, updated oil lines, dyno tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when engine has 90k+ miles or shows additional wear; eliminates repeat turbo failure risk

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 508 GT 1.6 THP valued at £10,000-£16,000, replacing the turbo is sensible if the engine is otherwise sound. For higher-mileage examples or vehicles with additional oil consumption signs, a rebuilt EP6 unit with upgraded turbo components offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP6DT Peugeot 508 engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 508
finalCta.paragraph: From EP6DT timing chain risks to DW10FC AdBlue faults, Peugeot 508 engine problems follow predictable patterns. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot 508 specialists for rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines. Every unit includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 508 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 508 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Peugeot 508 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L BlueHDi (DV5) debuted on Gen 2 508 in 2018 as the entry diesel, replacing the 1.6L DV6 with improved emissions while maintaining efficiency for urban and motorway use.
commonFailurePoints: AdBlue injector crystallisation
EGR valve clogging on short journeys
timing chain tensioner wear on early units

items.title: Peugeot 508 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HDi/BlueHDi (DV6) powered Gen 1 508 diesels from launch, offering proven economy with widespread parts availability across the PSA group.
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler cracking
DPF regeneration failure on urban use
turbo oil seal leak on high-mileage examples

items.title: Peugeot 508 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L HDi/BlueHDi (DW10/DW10FC) served as the torque-rich diesel option across both 508 generations, favoured for towing and high-mileage duty.
commonFailurePoints: High-pressure fuel pump wear
AdBlue tank/pump failure on BlueHDi units
injector seal failure causing rough running

items.title: Peugeot 508 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L PureTech turbo (EB2ADTS) arrived on Gen 2 508 as the entry petrol, balancing efficiency with adequate performance for urban and motorway driving.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt degradation in oil bath (critical at 100k miles/10 years)
oil pump wear on early units
turbo bearing wear on high-mileage examples

items.title: Peugeot 508 1.6L Petrol Engines

commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure on EP6DT
HPFP pressure loss on EP6FDT
turbo oil feed pipe leak
carbon buildup on intake valves

items.title: Peugeot 508 2.0L Petrol Engines

variable valve timing solenoid sticking

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 508 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 508's production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Gen 1 Launch: DV6 HDi & EP6DT THP Debut
years.description: The first-generation 508 launched with proven DV6 diesel and EP6DT turbo petrol engines, establishing the core powertrains for the model's early years.
keyChanges: Gen 1 508 saloon and SW estate introduced on PSA PF3 platform
DV6 1.6L HDi and DW10 2.0L HDi diesel options for economy and torque
EP6DT 1.6L THP turbo petrol added for performance variants
EAT6 automatic transmission optional on higher trims
mainEngines: EP6DT - 1.6L turbo petrol (GT 1.6 THP / First Edition 1.6 THP)
knownFor: DV6 EGR cooler cracking becomes common after 80k miles
EP6DT timing chain tensioner wear noted on early production units
DW10 praised for torque and towing capability on SW estate
Strong residual values on well-maintained Gen 1 examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Peugeot 508 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 508 1.6 HDi engine replacement • Peugeot 508 EP6DT timing chain cost • Peugeot 508 2.0 HDi DPF fix

years.description: The 2015 facelift brought Euro 6 compliance updates to diesel engines and minor refinements to petrol units, extending Gen 1's relevance before Gen 2 launch.
keyChanges: Facelift styling with vertical grille and full LED headlights option
DV6 updated to BlueHDi specification for Euro 6 emissions
EP6DT received minor calibration updates for improved refinement
EAT6 automatic became standard on GT and RXH variants
knownFor: BlueHDi AdBlue system introduced, adding new failure points
RXH Hybrid4 specialist interest due to unique diesel-electric layout
EP6DT timing chain issues remain dominant search query for Gen 1
Final production year 2018 sees increased used engine demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2018 Peugeot 508 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 508 RXH Hybrid4 engine • Peugeot 508 BlueHDi AdBlue fault • Peugeot 508 1.6 THP rebuild cost

years.preview: Gen 2 Launch: EMP2 Platform & New Engine Families
years.description: The all-new Gen 2 508 on EMP2 platform introduced DV5 1.5L BlueHDi and EP6FDT PureTech engines, with modern emissions tech and updated architecture.
keyChanges: Gen 2 508 launched on stretched EMP2 platform with liftback and SW body styles
DV5 1.5L BlueHDi replaced DV6 as entry diesel; DW10FC 2.0L BlueHDi for performance
EP6FDT PureTech replaced EP6DT THP with improved reliability and efficiency
EB2ADTS 1.2L PureTech added as entry petrol option from 2020
knownFor: DV5 AdBlue injector crystallisation emerges as common Gen 2 diesel fault
EP6FDT HPFP pressure loss noted on early production units
EB2ADTS oil-bath timing belt requires strict maintenance discipline
Gen 2 styling and tech drive strong demand for replacement engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2020 Peugeot 508 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 508 1.5 BlueHDi AdBlue • Peugeot 508 EP6FDT HPFP • Peugeot 508 1.2 PureTech belt

years.description: Plug-in hybrid variants arrived with EP6FDT PHEV and performance-focused PSE Hybrid, adding electrified complexity to the 508 engine range.
keyChanges: EP6FDT PHEV plug-in hybrid introduced for Allure/GT variants (225hp combined)
Peugeot Sport Engineered (PSE) Hybrid launched with twin-motor 360hp output
DW10FC and EP6FDT received minor calibration updates for Euro 6d compliance
EB2ADTS 1.2L PureTech became standard entry petrol across Active/Allure
mainEngines: EP6FDT PHEV - 1.6L plug-in hybrid (Allure PHEV / GT PHEV)
EP6FDT PSE - 1.6L performance plug-in hybrid (PSE Hybrid)
knownFor: PHEV on-board charger faults limit electric range on early units
PSE Hybrid rear electric motor coupling wear under high-torque use
DV5/DW10FC AdBlue system remains dominant diesel replacement driver
Gen 2 facelift (2023) brings minor updates but no new engine families
years.cta: Get quotes for 2021-2024 Peugeot 508 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 508 PSE Hybrid engine • Peugeot 508 PHEV battery fault • Peugeot 508 GT Premium 2.0 BlueHDi

faq.h2: Peugeot 508 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 508 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Peugeot 508 replacement engines range from £450-£1,200 for used units to £1,400-£3,400 for rebuilt, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit typically adds £400-£800 labour.
keyPoints: Used engines start around £450 for DV6 1.6L HDi units
Rebuilt EP6FDT petrol engines average £2,000-£3,400 supply only
DW10FC 2.0L BlueHDi rebuilt units typically £1,800-£3,100
Supply & fit labour adds £400-£800 depending on garage location
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 508 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Peugeot 508?
items.answer: Common Peugeot 508 engine codes include DV5, DW10FC for diesel and EP6FDT, EB2ADTS for petrol. Enter your registration or VIN for instant compatibility verification.
keyPoints: Diesel codes: DV5 (1.5L), DV6 (1.6L), DW10/DW10FC (2.0L)
Petrol codes: EB2ADTS (1.2L), EP6DT/EP6FDT (1.6L), EW10A (2.0L)
Hybrid codes: DW10 HYbrid4, EP6FDT PHEV, EP6FDT PSE
Use our registration checker for exact code match to your variant
items.cta: Get instant Peugeot 508 engine code compatibility check

items.question: How long does Peugeot 508 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most Peugeot 508 engines are dispatched within 24-48 hours, with supply & fit installation typically completed within 1-2 working days at approved garages.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines usually ready for dispatch within 1-2 days
Used engines may require sourcing, adding 2-4 days lead time
Professional installation takes 6-8 hours for experienced mechanics
Post-fit ECU adaptation and road test add 1-2 hours
items.cta: Book Peugeot 508 engine supply & fit with fast turnaround

items.question: What warranty comes with Peugeot 508 replacement engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 508 engines include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; used engines typically carry 3-6 months cover depending on supplier terms.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/remanufactured units: 12-month unlimited mileage standard
Reconditioned engines: 12-month warranty with mileage limits sometimes applied
Used engines: 3-6 month warranty typical, verify before purchase
Warranty valid at approved UK garages; confirm terms with supplier
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 508 engine warranty terms from vetted suppliers

items.question: Can I upgrade my Peugeot 508 engine to a more powerful one?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible on Peugeot 508 with ECU and wiring adaptations, but require specialist calibration and may affect insurance and MOT compliance.
keyPoints: EP6FDT can be swapped between GT Line and GT variants with ECU recode
Diesel to petrol or hybrid conversions require extensive modifications
Always verify emissions compliance for MOT after any engine change
Specialist suppliers can advise on compatible performance upgrades
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 508 engine upgrade compatibility

items.question: How do I know if my Peugeot 508 needs a new engine or just repair?
items.answer: Get a compression test and diagnostic scan - if multiple cylinders show low compression or major internal damage is confirmed, replacement is usually more cost-effective than repair.
keyPoints: Compression test below 100psi on multiple cylinders suggests rebuild needed
Timing chain failure with valve contact usually requires full replacement
Minor issues like EGR or injector faults can often be repaired separately
Our free quote comparison includes diagnostic guidance from specialists
items.cta: Get free Peugeot 508 engine diagnosis and repair vs replace quotes

items.question: Are Peugeot 508 replacement engines MOT compliant?
items.answer: All Peugeot 508 replacement engines supplied through EngineMarket include emissions certification for full MOT compliance when installed by approved specialists.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines tested to meet original Euro 5/Euro 6 emissions standards
ECU calibration ensures DPF/AdBlue systems function correctly post-install
Supplier provides documentation for MOT tester verification
Always confirm emissions compliance with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Find MOT-compliant Peugeot 508 replacement engines

items.question: Do you supply Peugeot 508 engines with gearbox?
items.answer: Engine and gearbox packages are available for Peugeot 508 at approximately 15% saving versus separate purchase, subject to variant compatibility and supplier stock.
keyPoints: EAT6/EAT8 automatic gearbox options available with DW10FC and EP6FDT engines
Manual gearbox packages more common with DV5 and EB2ADTS units
Compatibility verified against your chassis code before dispatch
Package pricing typically saves £200-£500 versus buying components separately
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 508 engine and gearbox package deals

items.question: Can I fit a Peugeot 508 engine myself?
items.answer: Supply-only Peugeot 508 engines are available for DIY fitting, but professional installation is recommended to maintain warranty validity and ensure ECU adaptation.
keyPoints: Supply-only pricing excludes labour; you arrange fitting independently
ECU adaptation requires diagnostic equipment for EP6FDT/DW10FC codes
Professional installation ensures warranty remains valid with most suppliers
Torque specs and fluid specs vary by engine code; follow workshop manual
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 508 supply-only engine prices for DIY projects

items.question: What if my Peugeot 508 engine code isn't listed?
items.answer: Contact our specialists to source rare Peugeot 508 engine codes - our UK network can locate uncommon variants within 3-5 working days nationwide.
keyPoints: Rare codes like EW12 2.3L petrol or early DV6TED4 variants can be sourced
Hybrid and PSE codes require specialist suppliers with high-voltage certification
Lead time for uncommon codes typically 3-5 days versus 1-2 days for common units
Always provide full VIN when enquiring about rare engine codes
